title: hopea malibato
intro: Hopea malibato, also known as Philippine ironwood or Malibato, is a large, evergreen tree native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Dipterocarpaceae. Its wood has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to be effective in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Hopea malibato are said to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as having anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. Herbal preparations of Hopea malibato can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
